transcript.text: So nice to meet you.
This meeting is being recorded.
Thank you.
How are you doing?
I'm doing well, thank you.
I'm looking forward for the weekend.
I'm going to go bouldering for the first time in the last two years, so I'm super excited.
Bouldering?
Where are going bouldering?
Just to one of the climbing gyms in the area, so I'm located in the Salt Lake City area, and I used to love it, but then I just had basically like a slight concussion.
And I stopped doing it for like two years, so I'm back, and back to my previous physical shape, and I'm so, so excited to be back.
Amazing.
Yeah, well, I'm happy that you're getting back to what you love.
That's cool to hear.
Yeah, and how is your week going?
Do you have anything fun planned, or any vacation time?
I am in San Francisco this week to meet with the team, so it's always good to connect with people.
That's awesome.
Love it.
Yeah.
What's your favorite thing about San Francisco?
I don't really have one.
I'm just here for work.
I'm always just here for work.
Well, thank you so much for scheduling some time.
It's really great to meet you.
We're excited about hiring for this role.
I'll start by just giving you a little bit of context on myself and growthx and kind of how I'm thinking about this role within the team.
And then I'll ask you questions, and then I'll try and leave some time at the end for, yeah, for us to, you would ask me any questions as well.
Sounds awesome.
Love it.
Perfect.
So I lead customer operations for GrowthX, which mainly means that I help shape the processes and set up the teams so that we can optimize delivering content for our clients.
And I'll

We leverage a platform that was built internally to build AI workflows that generate our content at scale, and that platform was launched in June, so it's relatively new, but growthx is really mainly a services org.

So this role as account strategist leads the sort of account strategic direction for our clients in terms of like, what kind of content we want to publish, how we think about like their presence in terms of SEO, and how they show up in AI search, and really setting kind of a long term roadmap for our partnership.
Can I just ask a quick question?
Do I get it right that this is a strategic role, and the goal is to guide clients?
So what content do you want to create based on your goals?
We will set that direction.
So we won't ask them that question.
We will tell them that answer.
Awesome.
And then, so this client works with, or this role will work with managing editors and content managers who will be developing.
Content managers are kind of working in the workflows day-to-day, generating the content.
And managing editors are sort of the project managers for day-to-day task completion, making sure we're kind of like on deadline, on goal, and doing a lot of the editorial work.
And then this role is like the strategic account manager.
But again, it's a services role.
And we think a lot about the, you know, delivering in terms of marketing, marketing strategy and execution against their goals.
Mm-hmm.
Got it.
So client gives the goals, for example, ramp client, because I was actually.
I talking about with Tucker about them because they're currently also in my portfolio in my current role.
So I was just when I see them on the website, that was a bit funny.
So for example, they want to like optimize their social media.
So they do give maybe a brief with the goals, and then we guide them.
So how we how you can achieve it, what we should do from the marketing strategy, and how we can optimize like post creation via AI as well as human touch.
Sure.
Yeah, yeah, that's pretty close.
Okay, great.
Well, let's talk about you.
Yeah, happy to.
Yeah.
Why don't you give me the elevator pitch on your background?
Yeah, so basically, I had spent the last five years in customer success.
And my path into the customer success and working on value creation started in completely different world.
I was a tourist.
guide in my early 20s.
And there, I loved creating the early value.
And also, it taught me pivoting on the fly and making sure that I can read the room.
So all this same value creation that I still adore doing in my success slash account management job, I learned working in the field with people when I was a tour guide.
And over the last five years, I had worked within different startups with different verticals of clients, automotive, healthcare, NGO, MarTech, FinTech, you name it.
Probably I worked with them.
And I led before like 1.5 million in ERR.
Currently in my current role, I lead 1.7 million in ERR.
And I have a portfolio of around 50 customers.
I've worked before with 60 customers, and usually it's mid-market and enterprise mix.
And I would say I'm really focused in my current role and last, I would say, two, three years on being the strategic advisor.
Not really like being I'm your support person or I'm just the person to please you.
This is not about me.
I'm here to help you to reach your goals.
So I'm going to ask you lots of questions.
Maybe if sometimes your direction is not ideal from what we've seen from your industry, I'm going to advise you for some alternative ways how we can reach your path to value faster.
So I am all about caring about customer results and how we want to achieve it.
And, of course, measuring it.
Because if you don't measure it, you don't have baseline and result.
It will be really hard to achieve and get some...
So why I'm interested in this role?

Actually, I told to Tucker, and after our interview with Tucker, I was actually thinking, maybe it was too harsh or not, but I truly believe that my customer, my current customer success role won't exist in the nearest two slash five years.
AI is reshaping the industry so rapidly.
Value delivery, value creation is changing so fast, and I'm really deeply passionate about AI.
I have lots of curiosity about AI workflows, and basically this willingness to learn even more, like how we can scale more, how we can automate more, and deliver success more with AI.
And this is why I'm interested specifically in growthx, because you are standing on the intersections of human value, human...
Advisors and specialists, as well as scaling it with AI, and you're also at the front.
So I would love to join this AI and human corporation and be there to grow your customer base and make sure that customers reach their desired results.
Great.
Well, tell me a little bit about LearnUpon and what you're managing there.
Like, you know, what do they offer?
Talk to me about the role.
So LearnUpon, my current role, I manage enterprise book of business around 1.7 million in ERR.
And LearnUpon itself is an LMS, learning management system, which is just a system where you have seamless access to your training.
So you have, for example, you as like some big company like Greystar or Rump, you have your training.
And then you upload it to LearnUpOne, and all of your employees or clients or both audience can access it seamlessly.
So this is just content delivery, I would say.
Any specific questions that I can specifically answer to your question?
Yeah, well, what was your, like, how did you approach customer success management?
What does the team look like that's supporting these clients?
I mean, Ramp, I'm sure, is a huge client.
So how did you think about managing them?
Yeah, tell me more.
So as I'm managing, like, 50 clients, I always have to prioritize.
And besides the help with the CRMs, like Salesforce, PlanHat, I always think of prioritizing by customer health, any issues, any goals, what stage of the relationship we have with the client.
And basically, of course, regular touch points.
So, like, with every client, we are...
Trying to build trust and trying to, I'm trying always to position myself, I'm your external part of the team.
So I'm here to help you to move the needle towards your goals.

So having that schedule of just monthly or bi-monthly cadence calls, as well as being able to sense if some issues or missing usage is happening, that I can jump right in and spend my time where, like, on Pareto principle, where 20% of time equals to 80% of input.
Mm-hmm.
Got it.
And so how do you do that?
Give me an example.
So basically, like, with shuffling priorities, I always try to make sure that everything, like, in CRM, like, I put all my notes to stay organized.
Like, currently we use PlanHat.
So, like, after every interaction with client, like, any, like, points, pain points, any plans, like, anything that I, that we discussed, it all stays in the CRM.
I also make sure to put the, like, on, I would say, like, Google Docs.
I also have my documents that help me to keep track of everything.
So, I make sure that if we had discussed with clients some, like, planning for the next six months, I have the account plan in place specifically for this client that we had discussed.
And I'm using a lot of results strategy.
So, the strategic document planning where we are discussing what are our main goals, what is our actual state, what are the metrics that we are tracking, and what's our baseline, what's our goals.
And how we are going to change the, basically our tactical results first, then those tactical result change will have any behavioral change.
So let's say example, so completions of training is really low for one of my clients.

And basically when we started planning, I was just asking, like digging into the data and asking like, so I see that during the last three months you had really the lowest completions in the last year.
And like, could you please tell me why?
And they told that actually they had some change.
And now the Friday when all of the trainings should be completed is the busiest day for their staff.
They are coordinating the volunteers and they do not have time to complete training.
So staff was overwhelmed.

And this not only gave me insight, and I helped them to reschedule and reorganize their training in such ways that the most of trainings should be done by Tuesday, but also just gave them lots of recommendations that we can do some longer due dates, like Thursday, Friday, as well as upcoming Mondays, that people have more time to get those bite-sized pieces of training, and spend up to like 10 minutes just to finish it.
So people have more freedom and more feeling of the time that they own their time.
And as a result, after this conversation, their completion rates rose by 40% in the next three months.
And customer was really like praising us on G2, because they were saying that you helped to identify the problems that we still haven't realized we have.
And this is something I love, just digging deep.
And asking why.
So I am this master of six why.
So six why framework.
This is, I would say, my main book or like just the way how I love working.
Awesome.
Talk to me about a cross-functional project that you've worked on where you had to work with people outside your team.
Yeah, absolutely.
So while working cross-functionally, I do believe that CSMs, account strategists, they are like glue between the teams.
We talk customer to product.
We talk revenue to sales.
We talk technical pain points to technical team and so forth.
So once our, like in my previous role, our management was really pushing towards higher NRRs.
And it was the middle of Q3, it was summer and like, I would say dead season for our agency.
So I started digging into the strategy docs and just trying to identify some features that our top performing customers had in common.
So maybe some recipe for success.

And when I was digging through onboarding documentation for some of our most successful customers, I realized that five of our most successful customers had adopted specific four features in the first 90 days.
And I just had like a few customers from similar industry in implementation with me.
So I just tested that and we tried to adopt those specific feature sequence with them.
And their first time to value was in actually two months when regular first time to value is usually four, four and a half.
Months, sometimes five, and basically realizing this, I just understood that this is really important.
So I put together the company-wide workflow, and also worked together with our product team, as well as marketing, to make automated alerts in our system.
So all of the customers can start utilizing specific this, specifically this feature sequence.

And it was all about working together, just trying to communicate like all those things that I've seen, like how can I tell it to like our leadership that this is what matters, and trying to show example by example.
So this is fresh customer in implementation.
We just did this.
This is what I suppose is a success recipe.
And they just skyrocketed their growth in like two months.
And just explaining, showing the examples, it got me those yes from leadership that I was allowed to start implementing company-wide processes.
And the help of our product team, of our marketing team, it was huge.
I would not really do without them.
So it's like you're trying to go far, then we need to work together.
And all of the collaboration really resulted in company-wide change.
We had adopted this expansion recipe feature workflow.
And basically in the next six months, NRR was increased by 8% from 102 to 110.
Wow.
So it was the result of collaboration and just like digging into data, but collaboration stands.

And talk to me about a time when, like, implementation or, you know, something was, you kind of gave an example, but I'd love to think about, like, how you drove results, turned a client around from when things were going bad and all the things you did to get there.
Yeah.
Actually, I had a fintech client, a very big logo in my book of business.
And for six months, every of our kids' calls was starting from some product feedback and product complaints.
They were complaining about placement of the buttons, different colors of the buttons, like, lots of very small things.
And six months before the renewal, they just told me, like, you need to fix it or we are leaving.
And that would...
Really hard company, ARR, they are a very big ARR account.
So my first panic response would be just to go to product and try to negotiate some button placement.
But something really felt off.
Those are a big, giant company in tech sector, and they are arguing about button placement and color of buttons.
And I just sat together with their admin team, and I really tried to understand what's happening behind the scene.
And they told me that their compliance team was breathing down their neck because they had no way of showing and proving effectiveness of LMS to their compliance team.
So they couldn't really connect results, maybe.
Maybe decreased costs or increased revenue to like training in LMS.
And this was the moment when everything started to make sense.

We, together with our product team, as well as our technical engineers, we specifically tailored like what sort of reports they can have to satisfy their compliance team, just to show specifically the compliance data their compliance team wanted to see.

As well, we worked together on step-by-step framework, how we can turn around current state with those indicators like 30% completion rates, 70% completion past due dates, and how can we make it in the most compliant way possible to minimize any risks for your compliance team?
you.
Thank
And we were having like lengthy maybe two-hour conversation, very productive one.
And in the end, we just had a step-by-step plan and supported by the strategic results framework where we had, okay, this is our current state.
This is our desired state.
This is our behavioral changes we want to see.
And this is how our behavioral changes will drive business outcomes.
Yeah.

And basically after working together, just being as a part of their team, like they never again, they mentioned any buttons and any different color of button or that we want not on the top right, but we want on the top left, this main button.
So it really like completely turned around our relationship and they not only renewed.
not, it's
Because this conversation started six months before the renewal, they had signed a three-year contract, and basically their NRR was increased by 10% a year.

So we have them since then as one of the strongest advocates for the platform, and they just recently participated in our annual conference, and they were presenting their use case and how they reached the success that currently exists with LearnUponHelp.

Okay, can you talk to me a little bit about how you use AI day-to-day, how you think about AI in your workflows?
Yeah, love this question.
And actually, I was really thinking, Andi, that you will ask this, so I was just thinking what I will answer.
And I have a great story here.
So with those...
Strategic results frameworks that I'm constantly talking about, for me, before AI era, or before working smarter, not harder, I used to spend like two slash three hours post calls.
So when we discussed everything with the customer, I need to put it in a nice way, all the main points for the customers to the framework.
But just around like six months ago, I started working smarter and not harder.
I just put to Claude, sometimes ChatGPT, my pre-filled framework, also the recording of the call with the customer.
And I was just asking, what's missing?
And AI is able to be my second brain, to make sure that I stay truthful, and specifically, we do not see what I understood they said.
But specifically what they said.
So this misunderstanding because of human brain, how you react to the information, like you only soak up to 7% or something like this, it was gone.

And all of my result strategy frameworks were not only done in up to 30 minutes instead of 2-3 hours, but also it were so much more to the point, specifically what customer told me, and not what I heard.
And customer, after I started this approach, they had highlighted that now you really like got all the points that we were thinking about.
You just like put our brain to this framework and to the paper.
And they're really happy.
And for me, it's just a complete time saver.
That's great.
Do you use it anywhere else?
Yes.
So basically, currently, with...
With our product, we do have some AI capabilities integrated where you can do the reflections.
And I was leading the project of incorporating AI just to making the customer base understand.
You know, like some of our admins are very far away from AI.
So I was leading the product webinars together with our amazing product team just to help them understand where do you get the value and cutting your management time fast.
So we had amazing like three webinars just like in the spring this year.
And I was happy just to help them to break down for the common workflows, how you can utilize it to get the best results and just to save time.
Also, I constantly use it.
I would say I would love, you know, like I love your mission to become AI native.
And I'm trying.
I'm to do it on my own, but I would love, you know, just to scale this with growth, growthx.
So I'm constantly using it for my personal use, as well as professional, like for professional use.

I'm trying to like make sure that if I have, for example, customers that haven't paid for the last 60 days, and our finance team is really trying to get a hold of them, I'm trying to like craft messaging with AI that specifically will spark interest of people who had been avoiding paying their recent invoices.

And it actually worked that somehow with AI, when you are just given the context, what you want to craft and why, that I want to craft this email that customer not only sees it, but it also will promote some response, and it really helped like.
We had been able to cover like several invoices just with this AI crafted and human touch combination.
For my personal life, I love using AI sometimes as like self-reflection and also as just a tool to help me to become a better version of myself every day.
Like I usually do like 15 minutes power session, like what can you random facts that you can teach me today?
Just give me something that I would not know otherwise.
And I love just to make sure that AI makes me richer as a person, like my mindset, as well as more effective and more, even more power value and power focused as a professional.
If you need more examples, because I did lots of blah, blah, blah.

Yeah, so I think there's two things.

Um, I, and so that, you know, you have to, there is no steady state.
uh, uh, uh, Thank

If it's performing well, or if clients are excited, they want more.
And we're a services company.
so we have to really limit what we're delivering.
Yeah, you have just to figure out where we can deliver the most impact with protecting the bandwidth of the teams.

Yeah, and like, another thing, like, why I'm so excited what I told to Tucker, I told when I talked to Tucker, there is so much space to like build things from the grounds to validate ideas, like in marketing, A, B validation, just to do so much of creation and building stuff from the ground.

So are we doing what we said we were going to do?
Are we, and then, you know, metrics in terms of, like, if we are and it's not performing, are, again, are we able to pivot?
Like, and how are we making sure that what we're doing is scalable?

Because I think like the account strategist is going to help us help me specifically think through making sure that we're not like just hiring more people as we grow, but that we're scaling processes and that we can become more efficient that way, too.
And like to what degree do you currently use AI and automations internally?
Do you have like any AI built frameworks or just like how like currently on this early stage AI helps to scale more efficiently?

Obviously, we use Fathom, which I guess is like theoretically AI.

Love it.
Already like, you know, proficient chat GPT and cloud user.

Awesome.

with...

Yeah, absolutely.
Love it.
